ddls:
CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_genres" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre associated with the film */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, project manager, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Specific action performed */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., dashboard, report) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film being reviewed */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date the feedback was provided */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating provided with the feedback */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cast" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cast entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crew" (
  "crew_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crew entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "crew_member_name" TEXT /* Name of the crew member */,
  "crew_member_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crew member */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the crew member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crew_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_expenditures" (
  "expenditure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each expenditure entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "expenditure_type" TEXT /* Type of expenditure */,
  "expenditure_amount" REAL /* Amount of expenditure */,
  "expenditure_date" TEXT /* Date of expenditure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("expenditure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "film_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* ID of the film */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_financials" (
  "version_financial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial entry */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the version */,
  "profit" REAL /* Profit made by the version */,
  "expenses" REAL /* Expenses incurred by the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_financial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_financials_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES film_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_critical_reception" (
  "version_critical_reception_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each critical reception entry */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version */,
  "critic_score" REAL /* Critic score for the version */,
  "audience_score" REAL /* Audience score for the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_critical_reception_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_critical_reception_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES film_versions ("version_id")
)
